operation: First, a workplane is initialized on the XZ plane to generate a base right cylinder with a height of 10 units and radius of 5 units, oriented along the Y axis. Next, the positive Y-facing flat end face of the cylinder is selected to create a new workplane on this face. A 2×1 rectangular point array with 4 unit spacing along the first axis and 1 unit spacing along the second axis is defined on this workplane. 1 unit radius circles are drawn at each array position, then a blind cut of 3 units depth inward into the cylinder is performed to form two partial blind holes.
geometry: The model is a solid right circular cylinder with two identical blind cylindrical holes recessed into one of its flat circular end faces. The holes are aligned parallel to the main cylinder's central longitudinal axis, spaced apart evenly across the end face, and terminate inside the main cylinder's volume rather than passing all the way through the part.
function: This component is designed to act as a spacer, vibration dampening bushing, or simple mounting block, with the two partial depth holes intended to accept fasteners or locating pins to secure the part to adjacent components in an assembly.
